perturbation.original_text: In the event a registration statement covering all of the Registrable Securities has -3- not been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on or before July 1, 2002, XC agrees to pay to AJR $10,000. XC shall pay to AJR an additional $10,000 for each thirty-day period thereafter (e.g., ending, August 1, September 1, etc.) in which a registration statement has not been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission for purposes of registering all Registrable Securities. All such payments due hereunder shall be payable on or before the 5th of the month in which such payment becomes due.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event a registration statement covering all of the Registrable Securities has -3- not been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on or before July 1, 2002, XC agrees to pay to AJR $10,000. XC shall pay to AJR an additional $10,000 for each thirty-day period thereafter (e.g., ending, August 1, September 1, etc.) in which a registration statement has not been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission for purposes of registering all Registrable Securities. All such payments due hereunder shall be payable on or before the 20th of the month in which such payment becomes due.
perturbation.explanation: The change modifies the payment deadline for the penalty fees. Originally, payments were due on or before the 5th of the month, but now they are due on or before the 20th. This creates an in-text contradiction because Section 3(d) establishes two different payment dates for the same obligation, creating uncertainty about the correct deadline and makes it unclear which date should be enforced, resulting in potential payment disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event a registration statement covering all of the Registrable Securities has -3- not been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on or before July 1, 2002, XC agrees to pay to AJR $10,000. XC shall pay to AJR an additional $10,000 for each thirty-day period thereafter (e.g., ending, August 1, September 1, etc.) in which a registration statement has not been filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission for purposes of registering all Registrable Securities. All such payments due hereunder shall be payable on or before the 20th of the month in which such payment becomes due. In the event XC voluntarily withdraws any registration statement submitted to the Securities and Exchange Commission, XC shall be deemed to have never submitted such registration statement for purposes of this paragraph. Unless otherwise agreed by XC and AJR, none of the Registrable Securities shall be sold as part of an underwritten public offering in connection with the registration statement filed pursuant to this paragraph, but, instead, shall be delivered to AJR as unrestricted, freely trading Shares.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment deadline for the penalty fees from the 5th to the 20th of the month. Since both the changed text and contradicted text are the same and both mention "All such payments due hereunder shall be payable on or before the 20th of the month in which such payment becomes due.", the contradiction lies in the fact that the original text specifies "All such payments due hereunder shall be payable on or before the 5th of the month in which such payment becomes due.". Therefore, this creates an in-text contradiction as different sections of the contract provide conflicting deadlines regarding the same penalty.

perturbation.original_text: AJR shall not take any action or suffer any action to occur, whether taken by the Team or others, which could result in an adverse impact on XC, its Licensed Materials and the goodwill associated therewith as a result of this Agreement. Neither party shall have the right to use the corporate name of the other.
perturbation.changed_text: AJR shall not take any action or suffer any action to occur, whether taken by the Team or others, which could result in an adverse impact on XC, its Licensed Materials and the goodwill associated therewith as a result of this Agreement. AJR shall be permitted to use the corporate name of XC in press releases pertaining to the sponsorship.
perturbation.explanation: The change adds a clause that allows AJR to use XC's corporate name in press releases, contradicting the previous sentence that explicitly prohibits either party from using the other's corporate name. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty regarding the permissible use of corporate names and could lead to disputes about the scope of the allowed usage in press releases or potential unauthorized use in other contexts.
perturbation.contradicted_text: AJR shall not take any action or suffer any action to occur, whether taken by the Team or others, which could result in an adverse impact on XC, its Licensed Materials and the goodwill associated therewith as a result of this Agreement. AJR shall be permitted to use the corporate name of XC in press releases pertaining to the sponsorship.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a specific allowance for AJR to use XC's corporate name in press releases, which directly contradicts the preceding sentence prohibiting either party from using the other's corporate name. This creates an inconsistency regarding the permissible use of corporate names, meeting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because different sections of the contract provide conflicting obligations. The first sentence states that 'Neither party shall have the right to use the corporate name of the other' while the subsequent sentence in the Changed Text allows AJR to use it in press releases. This is a clear conflict.

perturbation.original_text: The Indemnifying Party shall not, except with the prior written consent of the Indemnified Party, consent to entry of any judgment or administrative order or enter into any settlement that (i) could affect the intellectual property rights or other business interests of the Indemnified Party or (ii) does not include as an unconditional term thereof the giving by the claimant or plaintiff to the Indemnified Party of a release from all liability with respect to such claim or litigation.
perturbation.changed_text: The Indemnifying Party shall not, except with the prior written consent of the Indemnified Party, consent to entry of any judgment or administrative order or enter into any settlement that (i) could affect the intellectual property rights of the Indemnified Party. The Indemnifying Party is allowed to enter into settlement without the prior written consent of the Indemnified Party, if (ii) it includes as an unconditional term thereof the giving by the claimant or plaintiff to the Indemnified Party of a release from all liability with respect to such claim or litigation.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text contradicts the initial prohibition on settlements affecting intellectual property rights without consent. The new clause permits the Indemnifying Party to settle without consent, provided that the settlement includes a release of liability. This introduces ambiguity regarding settlements impacting intellectual property rights: one part requires consent, while the other does not, leading to potential disputes over settlement terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Indemnifying Party shall not, except with the prior written consent of the Indemnified Party, consent to entry of any judgment or administrative order or enter into any settlement that (i) could affect the intellectual property rights of the Indemnified Party. The Indemnifying Party is allowed to enter into settlement without the prior written consent of the Indemnified Party, if (ii) it includes as an unconditional term thereof the giving by the claimant or plaintiff to the Indemnified Party of a release from all liability with respect to such claim or litigation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction by initially prohibiting settlements affecting intellectual property rights without the Indemnified Party's consent, but then immediately allowing such settlements if they include a release of liability. This creates ambiguity and inconsistency, as the first clause states consent is required in all cases where intellectual property is affected, while the second clause creates an exception. This conflicts with the contradicted text in the same location, leading to an in-text contradiction as defined in Perturbation Explanation.
